hypotheses_tested.summary: Hold the accepted l9 = 0.8125 s ridge fixed and move the terminal l12 dwell halfway between the current mean-leaning leader (0.125 s) and the max-leaning neighbor (0.25 s).
Increase l9 only by one half-step to 0.84375 s while keeping the leader's lighter l12 = 0.125 s, to see whether dKg_xx suppression keeps improving before mean drifts.
Couple the same small l9 increase with midpoint / heavier terminal closures. This tests whether the max-oriented direction can be extended without reopening any new family.

rows_sorted.rationale: Keep the accepted l9 ridge fixed and place l12 at the exact midpoint between the current leader (0.125 s) and the max-oriented neighbor (0.25 s).
Micro-extend the accepted l9 gate upward by one half-step while keeping the lighter terminal closure unchanged, to test whether the current ridge is still climbing on max without sacrificing mean.
Diagonal in-basin compromise: slightly stronger l9 plus midpoint l12, aimed at absorbing some max gain from the heavier terminal side with less mean tax than the full 0.25 closure.
Most max-oriented admissible extension inside the same basin: raise l9 one half-step and keep the heavier 0.25 s terminal closure from the known max-leaning neighbor.

best_candidate.rationale: Keep the accepted l9 ridge fixed and place l12 at the exact midpoint between the current leader (0.125 s) and the max-oriented neighbor (0.25 s).

kf36_gate_reason: No candidate was close enough to the accepted leader to justify KF36.

required_comparison_rows.label: batch-best candidate / relay_r4_l9y0p8125_l12y0p1875_on_entry
required_comparison_rows.note: best point found inside this restart batch

bottom_line.statement: The local in-basin ridge remains centered on the accepted leader: this restart batch did not uncover a clearly better valid point beyond the already accepted corrected frontier.
